symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric palpable non-blanching purpura on lower legs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric palpable purpura on dependent areas is characteristic of IgA vasculitis.
Purpura can also be thrombocytopenic; requires a platelet count.
A well, afebrile, non-progressive leg-confined rash strongly argues against meningococcaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No abdominal pain, haematuria, or joint involvement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of GI/renal/joint features lowers organ-threat probability, but urinalysis is still required.
A preceding URTI is a classic trigger for IgA vasculitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day FBC (platelets), urinalysis and blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude thrombocytopenia and screen for IgA nephritis/hypertension — the key steps telehealth cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Renal function and urine protein:creatinine ratio
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess and monitor for IgA nephritis.
Not indicated in a well, afebrile patient without evidence of infection (retained only in the meningococcal safety-net).
